name: Esri ArcGIS Platform API
what_it_does: Esri ArcGIS Platform REST API for enterprise geographic information systems (GIS) and geospatial intelligence platform. Enables AI agents to manage geospatial data layer publishing and access control, handle spatial query and analysis automation using geocoding, routing, and proximity services, access feature service CRUD operations for geographic data management, retrieve map and scene service data for visualization and analysis, manage web map and dashboard configuration automation, handle geocoding and reverse geocoding for address and location data, access geoprocessing service orchestration for spatial workflows, retrieve network analysis data for routing, service areas, and logistics optimization, manage portal and organization content and permission management, and integrate geospatial data with enterprise asset management, field operations, and analytics platforms.
use_cases: Automating geospatial data updates from field operations agents
Performing spatial analysis from location intelligence agents
Managing GIS layer publishing from data management agents
Integrating location data with asset management from operations agents
not_for: Simple consumer mapping without enterprise spatial analysis requirements
High-volume map tile delivery without enterprise GIS data management
Real-time tracking without enterprise GIS infrastructure
best_when: An enterprise using Esri ArcGIS wants AI agents to automate geospatial data management, spatial analysis, geocoding and routing, field operations data collection, and enterprise system integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated edits to live enterprise GIS feature services can affect field operations and authoritative data sources — always use versioning or branch versioning for edits to critical datasets. Spatial analysis services with large datasets require proper resource planning to avoid service degradation.

auth.notes: ArcGIS Platform uses API keys (simple auth) and OAuth 2.0 for enterprise integrations. ArcGIS Identity (named user) for full feature access. Python SDK (arcgis for Python) for automation. ArcGIS API for JavaScript for web applications. Webhooks via ArcGIS Notebook Server. REST API with standard ArcGIS JSON format. Feature service editing requires specific privilege scoping.

pricing.free_tier_limits: ArcGIS Developer account free tier: 2M tiles, 20K geocodes/mo; enterprise requires licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: ArcGIS Platform by credits + ArcGIS Online/Enterprise annual subscriptions
pricing.notes: Redlands, California. Esri. Founded 1969. Private. GIS market leader globally (43%+ market share). $2B+ revenue. 350,000+ organizations use Esri products. ArcGIS Online SaaS and ArcGIS Enterprise on-premises. ArcGIS Pro desktop. Strong government, utilities, natural resources, and transportation verticals. Competes with Google Maps Platform and HERE for enterprise mapping.

performance.rate_limits: Credit-based consumption; ArcGIS Online has concurrent request limits; feature service query limits per service

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Feature service edits to authoritative GIS datasets require versioning — use branch versioning to avoid direct editing of production data
Credit consumption monitoring — ArcGIS Platform uses credits for services; monitor consumption in automation to avoid unexpected costs
arcgis for Python SDK — official Python SDK covers most platform operations; preferred for GIS automation over raw REST
Named user vs API key — API keys work for basemaps and geocoding; full feature access (editing, analysis) requires named user OAuth2 token
ArcGIS Online vs Enterprise — cloud (ArcGIS Online) and on-premises (ArcGIS Enterprise) have same API surface but different authentication endpoints
Spatial reference system (SRS) — specify coordinate system in all spatial queries; default is web mercator (EPSG:3857); match your data's SRS
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise GIS. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. API key/OAuth2. Multi-region. Geospatial, location, and spatial analysis data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise GIS. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. API key/OAuth2. Multi-region. Geospatial and location data.
